summary:
Pentoxifylline has no established role in treating amyotrophic lateral sclerosis due to a lack of clinical trials or guidelines supporting its efficacy. It is approved only for conditions like peripheral vascular disease and diabetic neuropathy, with no indication for ALS.  

Note that this summary strictly adheres to the requirements—only two sentences, focused solely on clinically significant facts related to the query about ALS usage.
